segments.text:
Hi, I'm Robert Curlee, technical product
marketing manager here at Sonar.

And today I'm going to walk you through a
new issue sandboxing capability inside
SonarQube Server that was released in
the new 2026.1 LTA.

This is what you'll see when you first
sign in to the server after updating to
the latest version.

And you'll get this prompt and it'll ask
you to turn on this new sandboxing capability.

You click turn it on and you'll jump directly
to the administration section where you'll
need to configure it and save your settings.
But let's take a look at what happens.

So after updating to the latest server,
you'll see that my project's quality gate
is still passed.

What happened is the issues that were found
from the new rules in the update did not
cause my quality gate to change to failed.

And instead, if I jump to overall code,
there's this new area called issue sandbox.

And inside here are those issues
that were discovered after the update.

When I click into one of the issues,
you'll see that it tells me that this issue
is in the issue sandbox.

And the status is in sandbox status.
This allows me to triage these issues
at my own pace at any time,

to decide when I want to switch them to open
or any other status and decide what to do
with them.

And that means my quality gate will only change
when I'm ready for it to change,
if I decide to move it to open.

So hopefully that'll help your updates go better
and you'll like this new capability

and start using it.
Thanks, and I'll see you in the next demo.

fullText:
Hi, I'm Robert Curlee, technical product
marketing manager here at Sonar. And today I'm going to walk you through a
new issue sandboxing capability inside
SonarQube Server that was released in
the new 2026.1 LTA. This is what you'll see when you first
sign in to the server after updating to
the latest version. And you'll get this prompt and it'll ask
you to turn on this new sandboxing capability. You click turn it on and you'll jump directly
to the administration section where you'll
need to configure it and save your settings. But let's take a look at what happens. So after updating to the latest server,
you'll see that my project's quality gate
is still passed. What happened is the issues that were found
from the new rules in the update did not
cause my quality gate to change to failed. And instead, if I jump to overall code,
there's this new area called issue sandbox. And inside here are those issues
that were discovered after the update. When I click into one of the issues,
you'll see that it tells me that this issue
is in the issue sandbox. And the status is in sandbox status.
This allows me to triage these issues
at my own pace at any time, to decide when I want to switch them to open
or any other status and decide what to do
with them. And that means my quality gate will only change
when I'm ready for it to change, if I decide to move it to open. So hopefully that'll help your updates go better
and you'll like this new capability and start using it.
Thanks, and I'll see you in the next demo.
